name: GLM-4.6
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts, 357B total / 32B active, 200K context (up from 128K in GLM-4.5), hybrid thinking/non-thinking response modes
note: Flagship coding/agentic update to GLM-4.5; expanded context to 200K, ~15% more token-efficient, near-parity with Claude Sonnet 4 on real-world coding (CC-Bench).

results.notes: AIME 2025, standard (non-tool) mode; Z.ai's own launch figure, relayed via Communeify/cirra.ai
LiveCodeBench v6; corroborated separately by CometAPI's write-up of the same launch figure
Terminal-Bench; exact harness version (2.0 vs original) not specified in source
tau2-Bench agentic tool-use benchmark, labeled 'T2-Bench' in source

Non-tool-use baseline score (30.4 with tool-integrated reasoning enabled), from Z.ai's official 'LLM Performance Evaluation: Agentic, Reasoning and Coding' chart for GLM-4.6, linked directly from the official Hugging Face model card (huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-4.6) as resources/bench_glm46.png. That exact file was later replaced on the GitHub repo's main branch by a GLM-4.7 chart; this URL pins the last commit (6fb1bbf, 2025-09-30, 'update the GLM-4.6 bench') that served the GLM-4.6 chart, and it is still fetchable.

Artificial Analysis's own IFBench measurement for GLM-4.6 (36.7%), verbatim-confirmed in the JSON payload embedded in BenchLM.ai's model page (AA-data mirror, display-only row, sourceUrl points to AA's own model page). Not reported by Zhipu/Z.ai itself; not currently tracked for this model.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real, released model (not a rumor) via Z.ai's own blog (z.ai/blog/glm-4.6, referenced but JS-rendered so not directly fetchable), Hugging Face model card (zai-org/GLM-4.6, MIT license, 357B/32B active, BF16/F32), and official docs.z.ai/guides/llm/glm-4.6 (200K context, 128K max output). Release date 2025-09-30 corroborated by CometAPI, docs.z.ai release-notes page, and multiple independent outlets (IntuitionLabs, CodeGPT). Params: HF card and llm-stats.com both say 357B total/32B active; some secondary blogs round to '355B' -- treated as the same figure, minor rounding noise. License: HF card and llm-stats.com say MIT; two lower-quality secondary blogs (codegpt.co, intuitionlabs.ai) incorrectly say Apache 2.0 -- MIT from the primary HF card is authoritative. Benchmark numbers (AIME-25 93.9, GPQA 81.0, LiveCodeBench v6 82.8, BrowseComp 45.1, SWE-bench Verified 68.0, Terminal-Bench 40.5, tau2-Bench 75.9) come from a table on cirra.ai attributed to a Communeify.com relay of Z.ai's own launch announcement; the AIME-25 93.9 and LiveCodeBench-v6 82.8 figures were independently corroborated by a separate WebSearch synthesis and by CometAPI's 82.8 LiveCodeBench figure, giving reasonable confidence these are the vendor's real self-reported numbers even though I could not load the z.ai blog page directly (client-rendered SPA returned empty content to WebFetch). Treated as self-reported since ultimately vendor-originated. Did not include CC-Bench win-rate (48.6% vs Claude Sonnet 4), HumanEval 84.2, or MBPP 80.7 -- CC-Bench is a proprietary Zhipu win-rate metric with no standard slug, and the source article itself flags HumanEval/MBPP as 'from a Chinese technical blog without independent validation.' Terminal-Bench version (2.0 vs unversioned) is ambiguous in the source; mapped to base 'terminal-bench' slug rather than 'terminal-bench-2' to avoid over-claiming the harness version.

2026-07-16 core-benchmark gap check (mmlu, math, humaneval, mmmu): all four remain genuinely unpublished for GLM-4.6 after checking (1) official launch materials -- Z.ai's own 'eight public benchmarks' launch chart (AIME-25, GPQA Diamond, LiveCodeBench v6, HLE, SWE-bench Verified, Terminal-Bench, tau2-Bench, BrowseComp), as relayed via cirra.ai/Communeify and corroborated by CometAPI, plus the Hugging Face model card (zai-org/GLM-4.6), which confirms none of MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/MMMU appear anywhere on the card by name; (2) independent leaderboards -- Artificial Analysis's GLM-4.6 model page shows only its 9-benchmark Intelligence Index composite (GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR) with no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval/MMMU breakout; Epoch AI's model page (epoch.ai/models/glm-4-6) shows only its Capabilities Index composite; llm-stats.com/models/glm-4.6 lists no score for any of the four; Vals.ai's model page tracks MMLU-Pro (not base MMLU) and did not render usable HumanEval/MATH figures. Sibling model GLM-4.5 (same family, released 2025-07-28, arXiv 2508.06471) did report MMLU 90.0 and MATH-500 98.2, but Z.ai's GLM-4.6 launch (2025-09-30) deliberately narrowed its published suite to 8 harder/agentic evals and dropped the saturated legacy MMLU/MATH-500/HumanEval trio entirely -- a deliberate curation choice, not a research gap. HumanEval specifically: the only number ever in circulation (84.2%, alongside MBPP 80.7%) is the same Chinese-technical-blog claim this file's original verificationNotes already flagged as unvalidated ('from a Chinese technical blog without independent validation'); no independent corroboration surfaced this pass either, so it stays excluded, consistent with the prior round's decision. MMMU is additionally categorically inapplicable, not merely unpublished: GLM-4.6 is confirmed text-only (capabilities.vision=false, modalities=["text"]) both in this corpus record and via search confirmation that Z.ai's vision-capable sibling is a separate later release, GLM-4.6V, with its own distinct Hugging Face repo and model card -- not this record.

2026-07-18 mmmu gap check: no new score added, confirmed architecturally inapplicable. Re-verified directly against this corpus record's own fields per the task's mandated check-first rule: modalities=["text"] and capabilities.vision=false, both set explicitly in this file. This reaffirms the conclusion already reached in the 2026-07-16 audit round (see prior verificationNotes paragraph), which had additionally confirmed via search that Zhipu/Z.ai's vision-capable sibling ships as a separate, distinct model -- GLM-4.6V -- with its own Hugging Face repo and model card, not this GLM-4.6 (text-only) record. Per the task's explicit instruction ("if vision is not a capability, propose omitted_no_evidence ... rather than searching further"), no further independent-tracker/arXiv/leaderboard search was performed for mmmu this round, since the model's own declared modality already settles the question: a text-only model cannot sit a vision-reasoning benchmark. Proposing omitted_no_evidence with reason "architecturally inapplicable (text-only model)".
